summary.intent: Address CI failures on PR #681 (TimeField implementation) in fogodev/ars-ui: fix a real branch coverage threshold failure in ars-components and handle an unrelated DOM browser test flake, while managing a multi-round Codex review cycle.
summary.outcome: Added branch-coverage-only unit tests to time_field/tests.rs that raised ars-components branch coverage from 89.6% to 90.88% (above the 90% CI threshold). The DOM browser failure was identified as a known flaky test unrelated to the TimeField diff, deferred for a job rerun after the workflow completes. Codex approved the latest commit.

repo: ars-components enforces a 90% branch coverage threshold in CI via cargo llvm-cov; falling below triggers a hard failure.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub reactions on the PR issue: 👀 means actively reviewing, 👍 means approved.

DOM Browser Tests (ars-dom: platform::wasm_tests::concurrent_animation_end_registrations_share_one_style_detection_batch) are timing-sensitive and can flake with zero style-detection callbacks when the expected count is two.
Commits to this repo require a 'Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>' trailer.

CI matrix includes: snapshot, formatting, clippy, workspace check, mutual exclusion, unit tests, integration, adapter, coverage, browser, release verification, Dioxus feature flags — unit tests and coverage are the slowest jobs.
cargo +nightly llvm-cov is the branch coverage toolchain; use --branch --summary-only for threshold-relevant output.
AttrMap is defined in crates/ars-core/src/connect.rs around line 2123.

code.finding: Only file modified: added branch-coverage tests for Context::PartialEq, idle/readonly machine guards, no-op helper paths, field_group_attrs/segment_attrs/root_attrs, and AttrMap branches.
TimeField implementation spans this range; branch gaps were in guard conditions, attribute builder paths, and service/context helpers.
AttrMap struct and impl live here; needed for coverage tests exercising attribute paths.
ComponentIds struct is defined here; referenced in TimeField tests.

workflow: GitHub API rejects gh run rerun-failed-jobs while the workflow is still in progress; must wait for full completion before retrying failed jobs.
gh run view --json jobs --jq with select filters is an efficient way to monitor only failed/in-progress jobs without parsing full output.
gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs piped through rg is the fastest path to triage a specific CI job failure.

When coverage is the failing metric, running cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --branch --summary-only locally before committing confirms the fix actually moves the number rather than just adding low-value assertions.
Pre-commit hooks on this repo can delay git push; the hook must be waited out, not bypassed.

friction: GitHub API rejected the DOM browser job rerun because the overall workflow was still running — forced a multi-cycle wait (10+ polling rounds at 2 min each) before the rerun could be attempted.

Identifying which branches were uncovered required three rounds of local llvm-cov text output parsing (awk + rg filters) because the text format interleaves files and the relevant section spanned >800 lines.
The Codex review pass took ~20 minutes of polling (10 sleep cycles) before transitioning from 👀 to 👍, with no intermediate signal.
cargo +nightly llvm-cov --branch --text output does not directly report per-file summary percentages; required a separate --summary-only report call to confirm threshold compliance.
open_items: DOM Browser Tests job (ars-dom: concurrent_animation_end_registrations_share_one_style_detection_batch) still needs a failed-job rerun after CI workflow 26416937640 completes — not yet retried at end of session.
PR #681 has not been merged; pending final CI green and any required human approval.
The root cause of the DOM browser test flake (zero style-detection callbacks vs. expected two) is undiagnosed — marked as environment/timing sensitivity but not verified or fixed.
